prerequisites: Identify the exact PyPI project name you want to pull, matching its canonical name on pypi.org (set as project_name). The JSON API is served from pypi.org and exposes published project and release metadata.

Optionally decide whether you want metadata for the latest release or a specific release. To target one release, know its exact version string (set as version); leave it empty to fetch the project's latest-release metadata.
Outbound network access to https://pypi.org. No PyPI account, login, or API token is needed because the read-only JSON API is public.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The PyPI JSON API is publicly readable and requires no credentials. The connector declares no secret fields, consistent with PyPI documenting no authentication requirement for reading project and release metadata. API tokens on PyPI are only used for uploading or managing your own projects, not for these read endpoints.

setupSteps.title: Set the project name
setupSteps.body: Provide project_name with the canonical PyPI project name (for example, the name shown at pypi.org/project/<name>/). This drives the JSON API request GET https://pypi.org/pypi/<project_name>/json, which returns the project's info, release history, and the files for the latest release.

setupSteps.title: Optionally pin a release version
setupSteps.body: Leave version empty to fetch metadata for the project's latest release. To pull a specific release instead, set version to that exact release string, which targets GET https://pypi.org/pypi/<project_name>/<version>/json and returns metadata and files for only that release.

setupSteps.title: Run the sync
setupSteps.body: No credentials are required. Because PyPI relies on heavy caching and CDN delivery, there is currently no edge rate limiting on these JSON API endpoints, so the connector can fetch metadata directly once the project name (and optional version) are configured.

sources.title: PyPI Docs - API policies (caching, rate limiting)
